message:
fix(runtime): add NULL/bounds guards to replay and snapshot subsystems

replay: guard asx_replay_submit() against NULL replay handle.

snapshot: add input validation to asx_snapshot_capture() (NULL output),
asx_snapshot_restore() (NULL/version-zero), asx_snapshot_diff() (NULL
pair), and asx_snapshot_compact() (NULL/below-minimum-threshold). Each
returns ASX_E_INVALID_ARGUMENT on failure. Header docs updated.

- 5 new tests covering all guard paths
- Close beads issue for snapshot hardening

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-ekb3","title":"EPIC: Hardcoded Constants, Stale Comments, and Code Quality Remediation","description":"Master epic for systematically addressing all hardcoded magic numbers that should be configurable,
stale/misleading comments indicating unfinished code, and related code quality issues discovered
during a comprehensive fresh-eyes audit of the entire asupersync ANSI C codebase.

## Background

A full-project audit was conducted scanning every .c and .h file for:
1. Hardcoded numeric constants in function bodies that should be named #define macros
2. Comments containing "will", "would", "TODO", "FIXME" indicating unfinished or deferred work
3. Stale documentation that no longer matches the actual implementation state

## Scope

This epic covers:
- Converting 4 hardcoded magic numbers to named, #ifndef-guarded compile-time constants
- Updating 2 stale header comments that incorrectly describe code as stubs/future work
- Identifying and documenting items verified as correct (not needing changes)
- Ensuring all changes compile cleanly with -Werror and pass existing test suites

## Architectural Principle

The asx project uses a "resource contracts instead of silent degradation" philosophy (README).
Every operational parameter should be:
1. Named (self-documenting in code)
2. Overridable at compile time (#ifndef guard)
3. Documented with its default value and unit
4. Consistent with the profile/resource-class system

This ensures embedded deployers (R1/R2/R3 resource classes) and specialized profiles
(HFT, AUTOMOTIVE) can tune thresholds without modifying source code.

## Verification

All changes MUST:
- Build cleanly with `make build` (strict -Werror)
- Pass all existing unit tests
- Not change any runtime behavior when using default values
- Be backward-compatible (existing code that doesn't override the macros gets identical behavior)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:02:45.202825037Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:16:14.362635656Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:16:14.362076177Z","close_reason":"All tasks completed: 4 constants extracted, 2 comments fixed, audit trail documented","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","code-quality","constants"]}

 {"id":"bd-va8n.1","title":"Create .clang-format config and install clang-format locally","description":"## Context
The FEATURE_PARITY_VERIFICATION report (Gap #2) notes that format-check and lint
gates report SKIP locally because clang-format/clang-tidy are not installed.

**CRITICAL FINDING:** .clang-format DOES NOT EXIST in this repository. It is not
merely missing from the local toolchain — the configuration file itself has never
been created. This bead must CREATE it, not just verify it.

## Background
CI presumably uses a default or inherited clang-format configuration. Without an
explicit .clang-format file in the repository, the formatting style is implicit
and non-reproducible across environments.

## Task
1. **Create .clang-format** in the repository root with a C99-appropriate style:
   - BasedOnStyle: LLVM (or Mozilla — audit existing code to pick the closest match)
   - IndentWidth: 4 (audit existing code for actual indent convention)
   - ColumnLimit: 100 (or whatever the existing code uses — measure before deciding)
   - BreakBeforeBraces, PointerAlignment, etc. — derive from existing codebase patterns
   - Add comments explaining non-obvious choices
2. Install clang-format (version >= 17) on the local build host
3. Run clang-format --dry-run --Werror on ALL .c and .h files
4. Fix any formatting issues, or adjust the .clang-format config so it matches
   the existing code style (config should match code, not the other way around)
5. Verify make format-check passes locally (no longer reports SKIP)

## Style Derivation Methodology
DO NOT blindly pick a style. Instead:
- Sample 10+ representative .c files from src/ and include/
- Run clang-format with different BasedOnStyle values, compare diff sizes
- Pick the style with the smallest diff (most similar to existing code)
- Fine-tune individual options to minimize remaining diffs
- Document which files were sampled and which style was chosen

## Acceptance Criteria
- .clang-format exists in repository root and is committed
- clang-format --version reports >= 17
- make format-check exits 0 (PASS, not SKIP)
- All .c and .h files pass format validation
- Style configuration is derived from existing code, not arbitrarily chosen

## Testing
- Run: clang-format --dry-run --Werror on all src/**/*.c and include/**/*.h
- Run: make format-check
- Verify zero diff between formatted and original code
- Run: clang-format --dump-config to confirm config is parseable

## Why This Matters
Without .clang-format, there is no reproducible formatting standard. Creating it
ensures all agents and developers produce consistently formatted code.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":45,"created_at":"2026-03-12T17:16:09.030106080Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T01:36:56.956616559Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T01:36:56.954820040Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-va8n.1","depends_on_id":"bd-va8n","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:16:09.030106080Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-va8n.2","title":"Create .clang-tidy config and install clang-tidy locally","description":"## Context
clang-tidy provides static analysis beyond what the compiler warns about. The CI
pipeline runs it as the lint gate, but locally it reports SKIP.

**CRITICAL FINDING:** .clang-tidy DOES NOT EXIST in this repository. It is not
merely missing from the local toolchain — the configuration file itself has never
been created. This bead must CREATE it, not just verify it.

## Background
Without .clang-tidy, clang-tidy runs with default checks, which produces too many
false positives on C99 code. A well-tuned .clang-tidy file enables useful checks
while suppressing irrelevant ones.

## Task
1. **Create .clang-tidy** in the repository root with C99-appropriate checks:
   - Enable: bugprone-*, cert-*, clang-analyzer-*, readability-implicit-bool-conversion
   - Disable: modernize-* (C++ only), cppcoreguidelines-* (C++ only),
     readability-magic-numbers (too noisy for embedded constants),
     bugprone-easily-swappable-parameters (too noisy for callback-heavy API)
   - Configure WarningsAsErrors for critical checks (null-dereference, use-after-free)
   - Add HeaderFilterRegex to only check project headers (not system headers)
   - Document rationale for each enabled/disabled check group
2. Install clang-tidy (version >= 17) on the local build host
3. Run clang-tidy on all source files with the project configuration
4. Triage findings:
   - Fix real bugs immediately
   - Suppress false positives with NOLINT + explanation comment
   - Adjust check configuration to reduce noise without hiding real issues
5. Verify make lint passes locally (no longer reports SKIP)

## Check Selection Methodology
- Start with a conservative set (bugprone-*, cert-*)
- Run on full codebase, categorize all findings
- If a check produces >10 false positives and <2 real bugs, disable it
- Document the triage in the .clang-tidy file as comments

## Acceptance Criteria
- .clang-tidy exists in repository root and is committed
- clang-tidy --version reports >= 17
- make lint exits 0 (PASS, not SKIP)
- Zero un-triaged findings
- Each disabled check has a documented rationale
- NOLINT annotations include explanation (e.g., // NOLINT(bugprone-foo): reason)

## Testing
- Run: clang-tidy src/**/*.c -- -Iinclude/ -std=c99
- Run: make lint
- Verify zero warnings/errors on clean code
- Intentionally introduce a bug (null deref) and verify clang-tidy catches it
- Remove the intentional bug after verification

## Why This Matters
Static analysis catches bugs that tests miss (especially UB, null derefs, type
confusion). Creating .clang-tidy with tuned checks gives meaningful results
instead of a wall of false positives.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":60,"created_at":"2026-03-12T17:16:20.496825071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T01:36:57.301315974Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T01:36:57.299330107Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-va8n.2","depends_on_id":"bd-va8n","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:16:20.496825071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
